sourcePrompt: An F-4 Phantom II fighter-bomber, about nineteen meters long, parked on tricycle gear with its nose high. A long pointed radome leads to a tandem two-seat canopy; broad low-set wings sweep back with their outer tips angled sharply upward, while the tailplanes angle just as sharply down - the Phantom's unmistakable cranked. Low-poly, +X forward, ground at Y=0; named pivots for moving parts.
